title: Planning, Dependencies, and Adaptive Decomposition · State Clinic: Critical Path
summary: Plan a migration whose dependencies and risks emerge during work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the loops critical path increment in a dependency-aware adaptive execution plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify serial dependencies and safe opportunities for parallel work.
Update the plan when evidence changes while preserving the goal and recorded decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Predict Critical Path
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: loops planning decomposition. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Build Critical Path evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: loops-critical-path-r4679x" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Critical Path into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: loops-critical-path-r4679x
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Critical Path model

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify serial dependencies and safe opportunities for parallel work.
